chapter_title.en: Chapter 38 – The employed and the guest

text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلٍ اكْتَرَى حِمَاراً ثُمَّ أَقْبَلَ بِهِ إِلَى أَصْحَابِ الثِّيَابِ فَابْتَاعَ مِنْهُمْ ثَوْباً أَوْ ثَوْبَيْنِ وَ تَرَكَ الْحِمَارَ فَقَالَ يُرَدُّ الْحِمَارُ عَلَى صَاحِبِهِ وَ يُتْبَعُ الَّذِي ذَهَبَ بِالثَّوْبَيْنِ وَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ قَطْعٌ إِنَّمَا هِيَ خِيَانَةٌ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Musa Bin Bakr, from Ali Bin Saeed who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about a man who hired a donkey. Then he went to the owners of the clothes (shop), so he bought a cloth or two clothes from them, and left the donkey (with them). So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The donkey would be returned to its owner, and the one who went away with the two clothes would be followed, and there is no cutting upon him, but rather, it is betrayal (of trust)’.
